summary: High‑energy spring ’78 set with killer Truckin’, Wharf Rat & epic Rhythm Devils
blurb: The show bursts open with a punchy Jack Straw and builds into a powerhouse second set featuring an explosive Truckin’ → Wharf Rat → Around & Around sequence, a soaring Estimated Prophet → Eyes of the World jam, and a rousing U.S. Blues encore. Jerry’s guitar is on fire, Keith’s keyboards shine, and the dual‑drum Rhythm Devils segment is a highlight. Recording quality varies, but several soundboard sources capture the performance with crystal clarity.
review: On April 12, 1978 at Cameron Indoor Stadium, the Dead delivered one of the spring‑tour’s most exhilarating nights. The first set kicked off with Jack Straw and rolled through tight renditions of Dire Wolf, Beat It On Down The Line and a fiery Peggy‑O before slipping into a high‑octane stretch of Mama Tried → Mexicali Blues → Row Jimmy → New Minglewood Blues → Loser → Lazy Lightning > Supplication.  The second set was the real fireworks: Bertha segued straight into Good Lovin’, then It Must Have Been The Roses gave way to an Estimated Prophet that blossomed into a massive Eyes of the World, complete with a 25‑minute Rhythm Devils percussion marathon. From there the band launched into Truckin’ that exploded into Wharf Rat and spun out into Around & Around before closing with a jubilant U.S. Blues encore. Reviewers repeatedly note Jerry’s “on fire” guitar work – wind‑mill theatrics, blistering solos and even standout pedal‑steel on U.S. Blues – while Keith’s piano and electric piano were lush and front‑center throughout the jams. The twin drummers, Mickey and Bill, drove the set with thunderous power; their extended drum break and the Rhythm Devils segment are cited as one of the night’s peaks.  Though a few recordings suffer early vocal drop‑outs on Jack Straw and Dire Wolf, the overall consensus is that the performance is a high‑energy, era‑defining showcase captured best by the crisp Miller soundboard tape.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Ten of twelve recordings are clearly positive, praising the guitar fire, epic jams and Rhythm Devils segment; only two are mixed with minor complaints about early vocal glitches. The strong, consistent praise across multiple sources warrants a robust +1.6 sentiment boost.

best_recording.reason: Crisp, excellent‑quality soundboard capture that highlights the epic Wharf Rat, killer Truckin’ jam and massive Rhythm Devils segment with clear instrument separation.

key_highlights: Epic Truckin’ → Wharf Rat → Around & Around sequence
Estimated Prophet into Eyes of the World with a massive Rhythm Devils percussion marathon
Jerry’s pedal‑steel work on U.S. Blues and soaring guitar throughout
Lush Keith piano/electric piano that shines in the jams

band_performance.Jerry: Guitar is on fire all night – wind‑mill theatrics, blistering solos and a standout pedal‑steel moment on U.S. Blues; vocals dip early but recover with vigor.
band_performance.Phil: Provides a solid, steady bass foundation throughout; mentioned only briefly but never falters.
band_performance.Bob: Delivers strong vocal harmonies and energy; no specific critique needed as his contributions are consistently positive.
band_performance.Keith: Keyboards are lush and front‑center – piano and electric piano sparkle in the jams, especially during Estimated Prophet and Eyes of the World.
band_performance.Mickey: Drums are heavy, driving the set with power; shines in the Rhythm Devils segment and extended drum break.
band_performance.Billy: Shares the thunderous dual‑drum attack; together with Mickey creates one of the night’s high points.
